slides.title: Needed Steps for NFSv4.1 Respecification Effort

chat.author: David Noveck
chat.text: when will meeting start.   I am stucjin select-devices ???

chat.author: David Black
chat.text: Meeting is running

chat.author: Chuck Lever
chat.text: Meeting has been open since 7:45am

chat.author: David Black
chat.text: @DaveNoveck - have you tried manual device selection (pull down menus) if Meetecho can't automatically find anything?

chat.author: Brian Pawlowski
chat.text:
Meeting issues
Please send mail to support@ietf.org to report problems related to:
Registration
Meeting Agenda
Audio Streaming
Meeting Network
Meeting Room issues (A/V, HVAC, locked doors)
Meetecho
Zulip
The support@ietf.org email address is closely monitored during meeting week, and constantly monitored while meeting sessions are in progress.
Feature requests
Please raise requests for meeting tool features, such as Meetecho, on the IETF Tools Discussion list by sending email to tools-discuss@ietf.org.

chat.author: David Black
chat.text: @DaveNoveck - If you're already in meetecho, there's a profile button to the left of the leave button- hit that, then try manual device selection on the next menu.

chat.author: David Black
chat.text: ... the left of the leave button in menu at bottom of screen ...

chat.author: Sorin Faibish
chat.text: @DavidBlack: send him screen shot

chat.author: David Black
chat.text: @Sorin - Doesn't look like he currently has chat access

chat.author: Sorin Faibish
chat.text: He needs to leave and reconnect like last time.

chat.author: Sorin Faibish
chat.text: @DaveNoveck last time you exit and reconnect to fix this issue

chat.author: Thomas Haynes
chat.text: Dave said: still stuck.  have discoonected and reconnected.  devices are working but it won't let me in the room.  Giving up.   Let's try to deal with this in an interim meeting.

chat.author: Sorin Faibish
chat.text: @DaveNovek try to reboot your machine. My last recall from last time

chat.author: Chuck Lever
chat.text: Some or most of the content will be available via the minutes; and the discussion can take place on the list

chat.author: Thomas Haynes
chat.text: Labeled NFS was an example of an editor and authors

chat.author: David Black
chat.text: +1 on Beepy's "carrying capacity" comment - can't deal with all of the 8881-related drafts at once (and neither can the IESG).

chat.author: Sorin Faibish
chat.text: SotinFaibish +1

chat.author: David Black
chat.text: Maybe, maybe not.  Make sure that there's a short cogent summary of changes section.

chat.author: David Black
chat.text: That was reaction to Beepy's comment on IESG running diffs.

chat.author: Brian Pawlowski
chat.text: Yes indeed. A VERY SHORT "how to read this document" - anything to help the reviewer, might be appropriate.

chat.author: David Black
chat.text: What does O(1) RT mean?  1 RT unless something unusual and rare happens?

chat.author: David Black
chat.text: Is MTI enough?  I would think client needs to understand all encodings that servers are using.

chat.author: David Black
chat.text: Splitting encodings out into separate docs makes sense (security area does this frequently), not sure that all of the others make sense.
